_readme: Owner-editable config for scripts/deployment_ladder.py. fomc_dates is a list of {date, note} objects covering BOTH days of each meeting; note is 'SEP' for meetings with a Summary of Economic Projections (March/June/September/December), else null. Populated from the Fed's official calendar (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm) as of 2026-07-15, and kept current going forward by the fomc-calendar-refresh job in .github/workflows/mm-tools.yml (scripts/fetch_fomc_calendar.py), which re-scrapes that same page every January. earnings_dates starts EMPTY on purpose -- this app never backfills calendar facts from model memory (MASTER_AGENT_PROMPT.md discipline). Until filled in, the earnings side of the blackout guardrail fails soft to a labeled warning instead of silently assuming every day is clear. hy_oas_blowout_level/hy_oas_blowout_21d_jump are fixed reference thresholds (FRED BAMLH0A0HYM2 is quoted in percentage points), not fitted -- same spirit as positioning_adapters.py's STRETCHED_THRESHOLD. sep_blackout_extension_days (default 0 = off) optionally extends the blackout by N calendar days after each SEP-tagged meeting's second day, since SEP meetings (new dot plot / economic projections) tend to be higher-impact than a standard decision-only meeting; set to 1+ to enable.
